description: 8-bit AVR microcontroller, 32KB flash, 2KB SRAM, 1KB EEPROM, 28-pin DIP/TQFP — the Arduino Uno MCU

key_performance.flash: 32 KB (2 KB used by bootloader on Arduino)

pins.name: PC6/RESET
pins.description: Port C bit 6, also active-low RESET. Pull high to run; pull low to reset. In Arduino, RESET is exposed via the reset button and ICSP header.
pins.requirements: Add 10kΩ pull-up to VCC. If using as I/O, disable RSTDISBL fuse (irreversible). Arduino uses a 100nF cap from RESET to DTR for auto-reset.

pins.name: PD0/RXD
pins.description: Port D bit 0, UART RX. Receives serial data. On Arduino Uno this connects via USB-Serial chip.
pins.requirements: Leave unconnected or connect to UART TX source. 1kΩ series resistor recommended if connecting to 5V UART from 3.3V system.

pins.name: PD1/TXD
pins.description: Port D bit 1, UART TX. Transmits serial data at 5V logic.
pins.requirements: Level-shift to 3.3V before connecting to 3.3V UART RX input.

pins.name: PD2/INT0
pins.description: Port D bit 2, external interrupt 0. Configurable rising/falling/level trigger.
pins.requirements: General GPIO or interrupt input. Add pull-up resistor for switch inputs.

pins.name: PD3/INT1/OC2B
pins.description: Port D bit 3, external interrupt 1, Timer2 compare B output (PWM).
pins.requirements: GPIO, interrupt, or PWM output.

pins.name: PD4/T0/XCK
pins.description: Port D bit 4, Timer0 external clock input, UART external clock.
pins.requirements: General GPIO.

pins.name: VCC
pins.description: Digital supply voltage. Powers AVR core, flash, SRAM, and digital I/O.
pins.requirements: 100nF ceramic decoupling cap to GND close to pin. Optional 10µF electrolytic on board supply rail. Separate from AVCC for best ADC performance.

pins.name: GND
pins.description: Digital ground. Return path for VCC.
pins.requirements: Connect to PCB ground plane. Decouple VCC to this pin.

pins.name: PB6/XTAL1
pins.description: Port B bit 6, crystal oscillator input. On Arduino Uno a 16 MHz crystal is connected here.
pins.requirements: For crystal: connect one leg of 16 MHz crystal with 22pF cap to GND. For external clock source: drive directly, leave other side floating. For internal RC oscillator: leave both XTAL pins floating.

pins.name: PB7/XTAL2
pins.description: Port B bit 7, crystal oscillator output. Connect other leg of crystal with 22pF cap to GND.
pins.requirements: Connect 22pF cap to GND. See XTAL1 note.

pins.name: PD5/T1/OC0B
pins.description: Port D bit 5, Timer1 external clock, Timer0 compare B output (PWM).
pins.requirements: GPIO or PWM output.

pins.name: PD6/AIN0/OC0A
pins.description: Port D bit 6, analog comparator positive input, Timer0 compare A output (PWM).
pins.requirements: GPIO or PWM output.

pins.name: PD7/AIN1
pins.description: Port D bit 7, analog comparator negative input.
pins.requirements: General GPIO.

pins.name: PB0/ICP1/CLKO
pins.description: Port B bit 0, Timer1 input capture, system clock output (when CKOUT fuse programmed).
pins.requirements: General GPIO.

pins.name: PB1/OC1A
pins.description: Port B bit 1, Timer1 compare A output (PWM). 16-bit timer for high-resolution PWM.
pins.requirements: GPIO or PWM output.

pins.name: PB2/SS/OC1B
pins.description: Port B bit 2, SPI slave select (active-low), Timer1 compare B output (PWM).
pins.requirements: For SPI master mode: drive low to select slave device, or use as GPIO. Keep high when not selected as SPI slave.

pins.name: PB3/MOSI/OC2A
pins.description: Port B bit 3, SPI MOSI, Timer2 compare A output (PWM), ICSP MOSI.
pins.requirements: Connect to MOSI of SPI devices. Also used for ISP programming.

pins.name: PB4/MISO
pins.description: Port B bit 4, SPI MISO, ICSP MISO.
pins.requirements: Connect to MISO of SPI devices.

pins.name: PB5/SCK
pins.description: Port B bit 5, SPI clock, ICSP SCK. Also Arduino Uno pin 13 / onboard LED.
pins.requirements: SPI clock line. Shared with ISP programmer.

pins.name: AVCC
pins.description: Analog supply for ADC, Port C pins, and analog comparator. Should be same voltage as VCC (2.7–5.5V).
pins.requirements: MUST be connected even if ADC is unused. Connect to VCC through a 10Ω resistor in series, then 100nF to GND. Optional: add LC filter (10µH + 100nF) for better ADC noise performance.

pins.name: AREF
pins.description: ADC voltage reference. When REFS[1:0]=00 in ADMUX, AREF pin is the reference. Left floating if using internal references (VCC or 1.1V bandgap).
pins.requirements: Add 100nF cap to GND to filter noise when using AREF. Do NOT drive above AVCC. Leave floating when using internal reference (but add 100nF anyway to prevent oscillation).

pins.name: GND
pins.description: Analog ground. Reference for AVCC and AREF.
pins.requirements: Connect to PCB ground plane. Star-ground or separate analog ground pour for best ADC performance.

pins.name: PC0/ADC0
pins.description: Port C bit 0, ADC channel 0 (Arduino A0). 10-bit ADC, 0–AREF range.
pins.requirements: For ADC: drive with analog signal 0V to AREF. Do not enable internal pull-up when using as ADC input.

pins.name: PC1/ADC1
pins.description: Port C bit 1, ADC channel 1 (Arduino A1).
pins.requirements: Same as ADC0.

pins.name: PC2/ADC2
pins.description: Port C bit 2, ADC channel 2 (Arduino A2).
pins.requirements: Same as ADC0.

pins.name: PC3/ADC3
pins.description: Port C bit 3, ADC channel 3 (Arduino A3).
pins.requirements: Same as ADC0.

pins.name: PC4/ADC4/SDA
pins.description: Port C bit 4, ADC channel 4, I2C SDA (Arduino A4). TWI/I2C data line.
pins.requirements: For I2C: add 4.7kΩ pull-up to VCC. Open-drain bus — pull-up required. Do not enable internal pull-up for I2C.

pins.name: PC5/ADC5/SCL
pins.description: Port C bit 5, ADC channel 5, I2C SCL (Arduino A5). TWI/I2C clock line.
pins.requirements: For I2C: add 4.7kΩ pull-up to VCC. Open-drain bus.

required_passives.reason: Digital supply decoupling — required for stable operation
Analog supply decoupling — required even if ADC unused
AREF noise filtering — prevents ADC oscillation
RESET pull-up — holds MCU in run state. Required.
Crystal load capacitor — value depends on crystal spec, typically 12–22pF
Crystal load capacitor — paired with pin 9 cap

application_circuits.name: Minimal Arduino-compatible circuit
application_circuits.description: VCC and AVCC to 5V with 100nF decoupling. 10kΩ RESET pull-up. 16 MHz crystal with 2×22pF load caps on XTAL1/XTAL2. Optional 100nF on AREF. Bootloader enables serial programming via RXD/TXD.

common_mistakes: AVCC MUST be connected to supply — leaving it unconnected causes unpredictable behavior even if ADC is unused
RESET needs a 10kΩ pull-up to VCC — floating RESET causes random resets
Crystal load capacitors must match the crystal's specified CL — wrong value causes frequency error or failure to start
Do not enable internal pull-up on ADC input pins — it affects ADC accuracy
I2C (SDA/SCL) requires external 4.7kΩ pull-ups — internal pull-up is too weak for most buses
GPIO pins are 5V — direct connection to 3.3V devices (ESP32, Raspberry Pi) requires level shifting
Maximum 40mA per pin, 200mA per port, 400mA total from VCC — avoid directly driving motors
Brownout detection default varies by fuse setting — set BOD level appropriate for supply voltage
Boot fuse bits determine bootloader vs. application start — incorrect fuse programming bricks the chip
SPI (PB3/PB4/PB5) is shared with ISP programming — attached SPI devices may interfere with programming
notes: DIP28 pinout. Arduino Uno uses ATmega328P-PU (DIP) or ATmega328P-AU (SMD TQFP). Default fuses: 16 MHz external crystal, BOD at 2.7V, bootloader enabled. ISP programming via 6-pin ICSP header (MOSI, MISO, SCK, RESET, VCC, GND). Datasheet: Microchip DS40002061A.
